items.title: Mizuho Says Historic Slump in Yen Is Defying the Rates Rulebook
items.description: The slump in the yen to historic lows is forcing investors to rethink one of their most widely used strategies to gauge the currency’s moves, according to Mizuho Bank Ltd.

items.title: Goldman Flags Up Oil Surplus Even as Nations Rebuild Stockpiles
items.description: The global oil market is set to swing back into oversupply as the impact of the Iran war fades and traffic through the Strait of Hormuz recovers, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc.

items.title: Sixth Street, KKR Provide BridgeBio $1 Billion Preferred Equity
items.description: Sixth Street Partners and KKR & Co. have agreed to provide $1 billion of preferred equity to biotechnology company BridgeBio Pharma Inc., according to people with knowledge of the matter.

items.title: Yuan Rally Bets Fade as Options Traders Turn Bullish on Dollar
items.description: Bullish bets on the Chinese yuan are fading, with options traders unwinding one of the year’s most crowded trades after the Federal Reserve’s hawkish pivot.

items.title: Iran War Delivers Massive Swings in Chinese Petrochemicals Trade
items.description: The war in Iran has rewired China’s petrochemicals trade, allowing plants to ease their glut of the key building blocks for plastics, rubber and textiles by boosting exports.

items.title: Philippine Church Group Continues Protests, Disrupting Manila
items.description: An influential religious group continued its protest for a second day on Wednesday in support of a senator allied with Vice President Sara Duterte, again slowing traffic in Manila’s main highway.

items.title: Kotak Reclaims India ECM Lead With Mega Deals on Horizon
items.description: Kotak Mahindra Capital Co. reclaimed the top spot in India’s equity capital markets league table for the first half of the year, putting it in position to dominate a year with the country’s largest-ev

items.title: Vimeo Parent Bending Spoons, Backers Raise $1.68 Billion in IPO
items.description: Bending Spoons SpA, which acquires struggling software businesses, and some of its backers raised $1.68 billion in a US initial public offering priced above a marketed range.

items.title: US Labor Market Steady as Stocks Gain
items.description: US stocks advanced on Tuesday as the main indexes notched their best quarter in six years and traders parsed the latest economic data. Bloomberg's Enda Curran has the latest. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Jardine Matheson Taps Ex-Indonesian Fund CIO as Country Head
items.description: Jardine Matheson Holdings has hired Christopher Ganis, formerly chief investment officer at sovereign wealth fund Indonesia Investment Authority, as Indonesia country head, according to people familia

items.title: Gold Steadies as US-Iran Peace Talks Frame Inflation Outlook
items.description: Gold was steady, with traders assessing peace talks between the US and Iran and weighing a fresh slew of US economic data for clues on the Federal Reserve’s next move on interest rates.

items.title: Oaktree Capital-Backed ITG Raises $312.2 Million in US IPO
items.description: Digital infrastructure services firm ITG Inc. raised $312.2 million in a US initial public offering, pricing its shares below a marketed range.

items.title: Shutterstock Shares Fall After Getty Says It Will End Merger
items.description: Shares of Shutterstock Inc. plunged about 30% in after-hours trading after Getty Images Holdings Inc. said it would cancel a planned merger due to a ruling from UK regulators.

items.title: Asian Stocks Slip After Two-Day Rally, Yen Steady: Markets Wrap
items.description: Asian equities edged lower as investors turned cautious after a rally in chipmakers and other tech stocks helped propel them to their best quarter in 17 years.

items.title: Oil Steadies as Peace Talks Continue, Hormuz Traffic Recovers
items.description: Oil steadied after the biggest quarterly drop since the pandemic, as traders monitored peace talks between the US and Iran and the return of shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Nike Appoints Pfizer CFO as New Finance Chief Amid Industry Experience Questions
items.description: Nina Trentmann, Senior Editor at Bloomberg News, discussed the recent appointment of Dave Denton, formerly Pfizer's CFO, as Nike's new Finance Chief. While some analysts have expressed concerns about

items.title: Alcoa Bets on Aluminum Boom With $5.6 Billion South32 Deal
items.description: Alcoa Corp. has agreed to buy South32 Ltd.’s bauxite, alumina and aluminum assets in a deal worth as much as $5.6 billion, cementing its position as a top producer as long-term demand strengthens and

items.title: Paris-Educated Economist Tapped as Colombian Finance Chief
items.description: Colombian President-elect Abelardo de la Espriella named Miguel Gómez to be his finance minister, tapping the former lawmaker from a conservative political dynasty to curb a rising national debt load

items.title: Progress Software Earnings Beat Estimates, Boosts Forecast
items.description: Yogesh Gupta, CEO of Progress Software, said that AI is a big driver for his company as it beat earnings estimates and boosts forecasts for the next quarter. Gupta said that his company can aggregate

items.title: Assessing Nike vs. Adidas as World Cup Heats Up
items.description: Drake MacFarlane, M Science Research Analyst, expressed cautious optimism about Nike's recent apparel sales boost, particularly from soccer jerseys, noting it as a positive short-term factor. However,
